quick_facts.label: Qualification
quick_facts.value: M.Sc. is a postgraduate science master’s degree. Under the National Credit Framework, the standard master’s pathway after a three-year bachelor’s degree is two years (four semesters); one-year master’s routes may apply after a four-year honours/research bachelor’s degree.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Entry normally requires a relevant undergraduate degree, but the required subjects, marks, and qualifying examination are programme-specific. For example, IISc’s M.Sc. Chemical Sciences requires a first-class B.Sc. or equivalent with Chemistry as a main subject and Mathematics at Class 12/PUC level.

quick_facts.label: IIT admission route
quick_facts.value: IIT JAM is a principal route to participating IIT postgraduate programmes. For academic year 2026–27, the JAM site lists about 3,000 seats across 105 postgraduate programmes at 23 IITs, with admission subject to each institute’s eligibility requirements.

quick_facts.label: Course design
quick_facts.value: M.Sc. study commonly combines advanced disciplinary coursework with laboratory or practical training and a research project/dissertation; IISc describes its two-year M.Sc. programmes as including foundational and flexible coursework, hands-on laboratory training, and independent thesis research.

quick_facts.label: Research progression
quick_facts.value: An M.Sc. can be a route to Ph.D. admission. UGC’s Ph.D. Regulations, 2022 govern minimum standards for doctoral programmes, while the Joint CSIR-UGC NET includes outcomes for JRF/assistant-professor eligibility and Ph.D. admission in science subjects.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: A relevant bachelor’s degree is the usual academic base for an M.Sc., but there is no single all-India subject or marks rule for every university. Applicants must check the chosen programme’s minimum educational qualification (MEQ), including required undergraduate subjects and minimum grade/marks.

For IIT JAM, candidates who have completed an undergraduate degree or are appearing in the final examination of their qualifying degree may take the examination; however, the undergraduate programme must be completed before admission and the admitting institute makes the final MEQ determination.

Programme requirements can be narrower than the degree title suggests. As an example, IISc’s M.Sc. Chemical Sciences specifies first class in B.Sc. or equivalent, Chemistry as a main subject, and Mathematics at Class 12/PUC level.
bullets: Match your bachelor’s subject combination to the target M.Sc. programme before applying.
Treat entrance-test eligibility and final programme eligibility as separate checks.
Read the institute’s current admission brochure/MEQ table, because requirements can change by academic session.

sections.heading: Duration
paragraphs: For the canonical Indian M.Sc. route following a three-year bachelor’s degree, the National Credit Framework describes a two-year, four-semester master’s degree. The framework also recognises a one-year master’s route after a four-year bachelor’s degree with honours/research, so applicants should verify the duration attached to their specific university and entry pathway.
IISc’s M.Sc. Life Sciences and Chemical Sciences are examples of two-year master’s programmes.
bullets: Canonical pathway after a three-year bachelor’s degree: two years / four semesters.
Some NEP-aligned institutions may offer one-year master’s routes for eligible four-year honours/research graduates.

sections.heading: Curriculum and learning format
paragraphs: The curriculum is discipline- and institution-specific: an M.Sc. in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Life Sciences, Statistics, or another science will have different core content. A research-oriented M.Sc. typically develops advanced subject knowledge alongside practical, analytical, and research skills.

IISc describes a model that includes foundational and flexible coursework, hands-on laboratory training in advanced techniques, personalised coursework/research projects, and an independent thesis research project. Its Chemical Sciences example lists areas including quantum chemistry, statistical mechanics, organic chemistry, spectroscopy, main-group chemistry, and organometallic chemistry.
bullets: Advanced core and elective papers in the chosen scientific discipline.
Laboratory, computational, field, or problem-solving work where applicable.
Research training culminating in a project or thesis in many programmes.

sections.heading: Admission routes and 2026 cycle context
paragraphs: Admissions are institution-specific. IIT JAM is used for admission to participating IIT postgraduate programmes, subject to the candidate meeting the relevant eligibility requirements; the JAM authority explicitly states that qualifying the examination does not itself guarantee a seat, scholarship, or job.

CUET-PG is another important examination route for participating universities. For example, the University of Delhi’s 2026–27 two-year PG process required candidates to have appeared in the relevant CUET-PG 2026 paper and used CUET-PG scores through its Common Seat Allocation System, subject to programme-specific eligibility.

Dated context: for JAM 2026, the additional/final seat-allocation round was scheduled for July 6–7, 2026, after which the admission process was to close. Candidates targeting a future intake should not rely on this completed cycle; they should monitor the next official JAM/CUET-PG and university admission notices.
bullets: For IIT routes: identify the matching JAM test paper, check institute-wise MEQ, qualify in JAM, then apply through the prescribed admission process.
For CUET-PG routes: take the relevant NTA test paper and separately register/apply on the university’s own admission portal when required.
Keep transcripts, degree/final-year evidence, category certificates where applicable, and other documents ready for verification.

sections.heading: Next options after an M.Sc.
paragraphs: A common academic progression is a Ph.D. in a relevant science discipline. UGC’s Ph.D. Regulations, 2022 set national minimum standards for doctoral-degree procedures, while individual universities publish their own admission rules, entrance tests, interviews, seat availability, and discipline requirements.

For science research and academic-track opportunities, the Joint CSIR-UGC NET is relevant in Chemical Sciences, Earth/Atmospheric/Ocean and Planetary Sciences, Life Sciences, Mathematical Sciences, and Physical Sciences. The June 2026 notice describes result categories connected with Junior Research Fellowship and assistant-professor eligibility, assistant-professor eligibility and Ph.D. admission, and Ph.D. admission only.

Other outcomes can include laboratory, analytical, quality, scientific-support, data, and research-assistant roles, but job titles and required technical skills depend heavily on the M.Sc. specialisation, practical training, local employer requirements, and experience. Salary and placement outcomes are therefore not stated as universal claims.
bullets: Ph.D. or integrated research pathways in the chosen field.
CSIR-UGC NET preparation for eligible science research/academic pathways.
Specialisation-aligned laboratory, analytics, quality, research-support, or technical roles.
Build evidence of capability through a thesis, lab techniques, coding/statistics where relevant, internships, and research projects.
